questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. Tanu said to me, “What a pity! You missed the party.”
options: Tanu exclaimed that it was a pity I had missed the party.
Tanu exclaimed that it was a pity you missed the party.
Tanu exclaimed what a pity it was that you missed the party.
Tanu told to me that it was a pity I had missed the party.
questions.explanation: Explanation: Tanu exclaimed that it was a pity I had missed the party. In indirect speech, "What a pity!" is reported as "it was a pity," and the past perfect tense ("had missed") is used to indicate an action that occurred before the reporting verb ("exclaimed").

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence have been Highlighted and given as options. Select the option that contains an error. When Raghav bought the book, he thought it will be interesting.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The error is in the word "will". The correct sentence should be: "When Raghav bought the book, he thought it would be interesting." The sentence uses the past tense "bought," so the correct form of the verb for future prediction or expectation in the past is "would," not "will."

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. The other half had been sued at least twice, and Levinson found that just on the basis of those conversations, she could find clear differences between the two groups. B. Recently the medical researcher Wendy Levinson recorded hundreds of conversations between a group of physicians and their patients. C. The surgeons who had never been sued spent more than three minutes longer with each patient than those who had been sued did. D. Roughly half of the doctors had never been sued.
questions.explanation: Explanation: B introduces the topic by saying Wendy Levinson recorded conversations between doctors and patients. D tells us that roughly half of the doctors had never been sued. A follows up by explaining that the other half had been sued and how Levinson could observe differences between the two groups. C provides an example of one such difference, saying surgeons who had never been sued spent more time with patients. Thus, the correct order is BDAC.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Memory like sieve
questions.explanation: Explanation: The correct meaning of the idiom "Memory like sieve" is "Poor memory." A "sieve" is a device used to strain liquids or separate solid particles from liquids, and because a sieve has holes, it can be used to metaphorically describe someone whose memory is poor or leaks information easily, much like how a sieve lets things slip through its holes.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the bracketed/Bold segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to improve it, select ‘no improvement required’. Abhimanyu said that (“She would have been a great sister if she is just a bit more careful”).
options: "She would have been the great sister if she were just a bit more careful"
"She would have been a great sister if she were just a bit more careful”
“She would have been a greatest sister if she was just a bit more careful"
questions.explanation: Explanation: The right answer is: "She would have been a great sister if she were just a bit more careful." This option correctly removes the unnecessary quotation marks and uses "were" in the correct subjunctive mood, which is appropriate for hypothetical situations. The sentence now properly conveys the hypothetical nature of the statement without extra wording.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The commissioner of Police was removed from his post as he was having a (clandestine) affair.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate antonym for the underlined word "clandestine" in the given sentence is: Overt Clandestine means something secret or hidden, usually done in a way that is meant to avoid detection. Overt means something open, clear, or observable, without any attempt to hide it. It is the opposite of clandestine. The meanings of other options are: Surreptitious: Secret, done in a sneaky way (synonym of clandestine). Amorous: Related to love or romantic attraction. Casual: Informal, relaxed, or happening without much effort.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined words. Due to the emergency visit to a hospital, Santosh has (a mental or emotional strain caused by demanding circumstances).
questions.explanation: Explanation: Stress refers to mental or emotional strain or tension caused by demanding situations, which fits the context of the sentence. The other options are: Reluctance refers to unwillingness or hesitation, not emotional strain. Penalty refers to a punishment or consequence, which is unrelated. Depression refers to a prolonged state of sadness or low mood, which is not the same as stress.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate Synonym of the given word. Insidious
questions.explanation: Explanation: Insidious means something that is harmful or dangerous in a subtle or gradual way, often without being noticed. So, "harmful" best fits as the synonym. The meanings of other options are: Weak refers to a lack of strength, which is unrelated. Protected means shielded or safe, the opposite of insidious. Derogatory means showing a critical or disrespectful attitude, which is not the meaning of insidious.

questions.question: The following sentences have been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. He behaves so aggressive in his new / home that he is rehoused with an old / couple who could give him everything he Lacks.
options: couple who could give him everything he lacks.
home that he is rehoused with an old
He behaves so aggressive in his new
questions.explanation: Explanation: The part that contains the error is: He behaves so aggressive in his new Correction: The correct form should be "He behaves so aggressively in his new". "Aggressive" is an adjective, but in this sentence, we need an adverb to describe the verb "behaves." Therefore, "aggressively" is the correct choice.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. She said to me, "The charlatan will be breaking into the safe."
options: She told me that the charlatan would be breaking into the safe.
She said to me that the charlatan will be breaking in the safe.
She said to me that the charlatan can be breaking into the safe.
She said to me that the charlatan is breaking into the safe.
questions.explanation: Explanation: In indirect speech, we generally change the tense from direct speech. "Will" (future tense) in direct speech changes to "would" (future in the past) in indirect speech. The rest of the sentence remains largely unchanged except for minor adjustments like "said to me" becoming "told me" to fit the indirect speech structure. The correct option is: She told me that the charlatan would be breaking into the safe.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active/passive voice. Who stole my tickets?
options: My tickets was stolen by whom?
My tickets got stolen by who?
By whom were my tickets stolen?
My tickets were stolen by who?
questions.explanation: Explanation: In passive voice, the object (my tickets) becomes the subject of the sentence. The question word "who" changes to "whom" in formal passive. The correct option is: By whom were my tickets stolen?

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Beauty of it
questions.explanation: Explanation: The idiom "beauty of it" generally refers to the positive aspects or the good points of a situation or thing. It highlights the attractive or admirable features of something. The correct answer is: Good points

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelled word.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The incorrectly spelled word is: Indispensible The correct spelling is Indispensable, which means absolutely necessary or essential. The meanings of other words are: Paraphernalia: Refers to the equipment, tools, or items used for a specific activity or purpose, often associated with a particular task or hobby. Handkerchiefs: Small pieces of cloth or fabric used for wiping the face, hands, or nose. Conjuror: A person who performs magic tricks or illusions, typically as a profession, also known as a magician.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. No matter how many times I try, I always end up in the same ______.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The phrase "in a pickle" is an idiom that means being in a difficult or troublesome situation. So the sentence would be: "No matter how many times I try, I always end up in the same pickle."

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. I was taking a stroll along the beach when I came across a heap of plastic waste. B. Realising the importance of removing the waste, I joined them C. Some children came to clean the beach, seeing the dumped waste. D. The adjacent industry had dumped the waste, which was floating, into the water, therefore polluting the seawater.
questions.explanation: Explanation: A: "I was taking a stroll along the beach when I came across a heap of plastic waste." D: "The adjacent industry had dumped the waste, which was floating, into the water, therefore polluting the seawater." C: "Some children came to clean the beach, seeing the dumped waste." B: "Realising the importance of removing the waste, I joined them." So, the correct answer is ADCB.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Bewitching
questions.explanation: Explanation: Bewitching means captivating or enchanting, often in a way that draws someone in. Alluring also means something that is very attractive or charming, which is similar to the meaning of bewitching. The meanings of other options are: Painful: Causing physical or emotional discomfort. Rude: Impolite or disrespectful in manner. Repulsive: Causing strong feelings of dislike or disgust.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. A person must speak eloquently and ______ while making a speech.
questions.explanation: Explanation: When making a speech, it is important to speak both eloquently and politely to effectively engage and respect the audience. Therefore, Politely is the most suitable choice to complete the sentence.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined words. A person who does not drink Alcohol.
questions.explanation: Explanation: A person who does not drink alcohol is called a teetotaller. This term refers to someone who abstains completely from alcoholic beverages. The other options are: Vegetarian: A person who does not consume meat. Celibate: A person who abstains from marriage and sexual relations, often for religious reasons. Abstain: To deliberately avoid or refrain from something.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active/passive voice. The Monkey ate all the pastries.
options: All the pastries is eaten by the Monkey.
All the pastries were eaten by the Monkey.
All the pastries had been eaten by the Monkey.
All the pastries are eaten by the Monkey.
questions.explanation: Explanation: Active Voice: The monkey (subject) ate (verb) all the pastries (object). Passive Voice: All the pastries (subject) were eaten (verb) by the monkey (agent). This transformation maintains the original meaning while shifting the focus from the doer (the monkey) to the action and the recipient (the pastries). Therefore, the correct option is: All the pastries were eaten by the monkey.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Maladroit
questions.explanation: Explanation: The word "maladroit" means lacking skill or being clumsy. Therefore, the most appropriate antonym is "skillful," which means having or showing the ability to do something well. The meanings of other options are: Arrogant: Having or revealing an exaggerated sense of one's own importance or abilities. Inept: Having or showing no skill; clumsy. Awkward: Lacking skill or dexterity; clumsy.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Nainital has a (1) _______ lake. Tourists come from far and wide to (2) ______ boating here. There is a skating place behind it. People who skate here say that the ride seems to go round with them. Evenings are also pleasant here. The (3) ______ of the electric lights look as if they are dancing in the lake's ripples. Truly, Nainital is a/an (4) _______ city of (5) _______ beauty. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank 1 is "beautiful". So, the complete sentence would be: Nainital has a beautiful lake.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Nainital has a (1) _______ lake. Tourists come from far and wide to (2) ______ boating here. There is a skating place behind it. People who skate here say that the ride seems to go round with them. Evenings are also pleasant here. The (3) ______ of the electric lights look as if they are dancing in the lake's ripples. Truly, Nainital is a/an (4) _______ city of (5) _______ beauty. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank 2 is "enjoy". So, the complete sentence would be: Tourists come from far and wide to enjoy boating here.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Nainital has a (1) _______ lake. Tourists come from far and wide to (2) ______ boating here. There is a skating place behind it. People who skate here say that the ride seems to go round with them. Evenings are also pleasant here. The (3) ______ of the electric lights look as if they are dancing in the lake's ripples. Truly, Nainital is a/an (4) _______ city of (5) _______ beauty. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank 3 is "reflection". So, the complete sentence would be: The reflection of the electric lights look as if they are dancing in the ripples of the lake.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Nainital has a (1) _______ lake. Tourists come from far and wide to (2) ______ boating here. There is a skating place behind it. People who skate here say that the ride seems to go round with them. Evenings are also pleasant here. The (3) ______ of the electric lights look as if they are dancing in the lake's ripples. Truly, Nainital is a/an (4) _______ city of (5) _______ beauty. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The correct option for blank 4 is "picturesque". "Nainital is a picturesque city of natural beauty." because it means "visually attractive, charming, or scenic, like a picture."

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Nainital has a (1) _______ lake. Tourists come from far and wide to (2) ______ boating here. There is a skating place behind it. People who skate here say that the ride seems to go round with them. Evenings are also pleasant here. The (3) ______ of the electric lights look as if they are dancing in the lake's ripples. Truly, Nainital is a/an (4) _______ city of (5) _______ beauty. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The passage describes the beauty of Nainital, and "natural beauty" is a common phrase that conveys the idea of a place's inherent, untouched, or scenic beauty. So, the correct sentence would be: Truly, Nainital is an excellent city of natural beauty.
